title: [ExportVerilog] Struct assignment patterns not supported for all tools
body:
E.g. iverilog https://github.com/steveicarus/iverilog/issues/555

Such a pattern is generated when a module has struct outputs:
```mlir
!T = !hw.struct<a: i32>
hw.module @packed_struct_assignment() -> (out: !T) {
    %c0_i32 = hw.constant 32 : i32
    %0 = hw.struct_create (%c0_i32) : !T
    hw.output %0 : !T
}
```

exports:
```sv
module packed_struct_assignment(
  output struct packed {logic [31:0] a; } out);

  assign out = '{a: (32'h20)};
endmodule
```

An alternative pattern that is generally supported (which could be enabled by a lowering option) is to break up the assignment:
```sv
module packed_struct_assignment(
  output struct packed {logic [31:0] a; } out);

  assign out.a = 32'h20;
endmodule
```
